sections.Consequences:
- Better auditability and reproducibility
- Requires discipline to keep records updated
sections.Context: Decisions and rationale are scattered across commits and chats.
sections.Decision: Store Decision Records (DRs) as Markdown + YAML in the repository.
sections.Rationale: Git-native artifacts reduce friction and improve traceability.

title: Introduce decision tracking artifacts in repo

sections.Alternatives:
1) CNN text classifier
2) BiLSTM with attention
sections.Consequences:
- Higher training cost
- More hyperparameters to tune
sections.Context: Existing baseline underperforms on minority classes.
sections.Decision: Use a Transformer encoder as the baseline architecture.
sections.Rationale: Better long-range dependency handling should improve minority-class performance.

title: Adopt Transformer encoder as baseline model

sections.Consequences:
- Results become comparable across runs
- Older baselines need re-running for fair comparison
sections.Context: Evaluation results were unstable due to class imbalance and small test set.
sections.Decision: Use stratified 70/30 split with a fixed seed (42) for dataset dvc:3f2c9a1.
sections.Rationale: Stabilizes metric estimates while preserving enough training data.

title: Switch evaluation to stratified 70/30 split with fixed seed

sections.Consequences:
- More explicit lineage
- Requires maintaining dataset versioning discipline
sections.Context: Data changes over time and evaluations become non-comparable.
sections.Decision: Always reference a concrete dataset version/hash in evaluation decisions.
sections.Rationale: Prevents silent data drift during experimentation.

title: Pin dataset version for all evaluations

sections.Alternatives:
1) Keep DR-0004 as policy-only
2) Add a lint rule without template changes
sections.Consequences:
- More consistent decision capture
- Slightly more upfront documentation in eval decisions
sections.Context: Generic rules were not consistently applied in practice.
sections.Decision: Make dataset reference a mandatory field in the evaluation_protocol template.
sections.Rationale: Template enforcement reduces omissions compared to a separate generic rule.

title: Replace generic dataset pin rule with evaluation_protocol template requirement

sections.Consequences:
- Potentially more iteration before acceptance
- Might slow deployment if improvements are hard to achieve
sections.Context: Recent runs suggest 0.75 is too lenient for deployment expectations.
sections.Decision: Propose raising the acceptance threshold to 0.78 for F1.
sections.Rationale: Higher threshold reduces risk of deploying underperforming models.

title: Adjust F1 acceptance threshold from 0.75 to 0.78
